learned_facts.fact: Dead code detection: start with static analysis (unused imports, unreachable branches), then verify with runtime coverage data; beware of reflection/dynamic dispatch hiding references; remove in small PRs with clear rollback path

Code evolution patterns: identify accretion layers (features added over time without refactoring); look for inconsistent naming conventions indicating different authors/eras; map dependency age to identify modernization candidates

Technical debt classification: distinguish between deliberate debt (conscious tradeoffs with documented rationale) and accidental debt (accumulated from ignorance or time pressure); prioritize debt on hot paths (frequently modified files)

Legacy system mapping: create dependency diagrams (module-level, not line-level); identify domain boundaries that align with business capabilities; find the load-bearing modules (high fan-in, everything depends on them) -- these need the most careful handling

Temperature configuration is now live (V_3.0.0). Code archaeologist runs at temperature 0.20 (analysis accuracy). Per-agent temperatures set in agent_config.json under model_params.temperature. Priority chain: caller-supplied > agent config > task routing > global default (0.4) > Ollama default (0.8). Reference: docs/tools/llm-router.md.

Python docstring conventions: Sphinx/reStructuredText style uses :param name: description for parameters. Google style uses Args: with indented parameters. NumPy style uses a Parameters section with dashed underlines. When asked about :param syntax, the answer is Sphinx/reST, NOT NumPy.

Martin Fowler technical debt quadrant: Deliberate Prudent = knowingly taking shortcuts with full understanding of tradeoffs (ship now, refactor later). Deliberate Reckless = knowingly cutting corners without caring. Inadvertent Prudent = learning after the fact that there was a better approach. Inadvertent Reckless = not knowing best practices.

Reaching definitions analysis: a definition d of variable v at point p reaches point q if there EXISTS A PATH from p to q along which d is NOT KILLED by another definition of v. The variable does NOT need to be used at q. The path does NOT need to be direct. Existence of any un-killed path is sufficient.

Mermaid flowchart arrow syntax: --> is solid line with arrow (standard). --- is solid line without arrow. ==> is thick line with arrow. ---> is a LONGER solid line with arrow (extra dashes increase length, NOT change arrow type). For standard solid arrow from A to B, use A --> B.

CFG cyclomatic complexity: CC = E - N + 2P where E = edges, N = nodes, P = connected components (usually 1). Count edges carefully: each branch (if/else) adds 2 edges (true + false). Return/exit edges count. For a function with 2 branch points, typical CC = 3.

Live variable analysis: a variable is live at a point if there exists a path from that point to a USE of the variable without an intervening redefinition. After a for loop completes, the loop variable is dead if no subsequent code references it. Variables used only inside the loop body are dead after the loop exit.
